text: Handling deadlines is a key skill in both academic and professional settings. Good time management helps you break large tasks into smaller, manageable steps. Prioritization is also essential—focus on the most urgent and important tasks first, and don’t be afraid to set less critical work aside. Scope management means understanding what is realistically possible within the given time, so avoid adding extra tasks that could delay completion. Finally, communication is vital. If you foresee a problem with a deadline, let the relevant people know early. By combining these strategies, you can reduce stress and consistently meet your commitments.
